name: Candied Apples/Halloween
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove stems from apples, wash, and pat dry.
2. Insert a wooden skewer in each apple, running through the apple from stem end to base without protruding all the way through the bottom end.
3. Combine sugar, corn syrup, cinnamon candies, and water in a medium-sized saucepan.
4. Cook until the candies dissolve, stirring constantly. Be careful not to boil.
5. Add cinnamon, vanilla, cloves, and food coloring. Mix thoroughly.
6. Boil the mixture to 300 degrees using a candy thermometer without stirring.
7. While the mixture is boiling, generously prepare a baking sheet with cooking spray so it's ready ahead of time.
8. As soon as the mixture reaches 300 degrees, remove it from heat and quickly dip each apple— one by one — into the mixture until it is thoroughly coated.
9. Set the coated apples, standing on their bottoms with skewer pointing up, on the baking sheet until the mixture hardens.
10. Let the apples reach room temperature before eating.
